programme.title: Jewels of the Med
programme.desc: Hvar is the longest and sunniest island in Croatia. Home to over 10,000 residents, known for its fields of lavender, rare and remarkable lace making, its secluded bays with beautiful beaches and six Unesco heritage listings. It's an island associated with luxury and beauty, a magnet for the rich and the famous. One of the island's most glamorous hangouts can be found in the capital, owned by Ivan Gospodnetic, and renowned for its seafood. Each day Ivan sends out a fleet of seven fishing boats. In 1868, Hvar became the birthplace of organised tourism in Europe which saw an explosion in popularity in the 1960s thanks to The Hvar Hygienic Society, a group who promoted the island as a health retreat. The Hvar Summer Festival is one of the oldest summer festivals on the Adriatic coast. This year marks the first time Jelena oversees the running of the festival's programme. Narrated by Joanna Lumley. Exec Prods: Dympna Jackson/ Melanie Darlaston; Purple Productions/GroupM Motion Entertainment Limited
Joanna Lumley narrates this exploration of Mediterranean islands, cuisine and culture.

programme.title: Jane McDonald: From Pole to Pole

Exec Prods: Kieron Collins/Mark Powell; Dir: Jenny MacLeod; JetSetTV Ltd (Ep6/6)
